title: Joby to Begin U.S. Operations in 2026 Under White House Air Taxi Program
text: Joby Aviation has been selected as a partner in the White House-backed eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP). The company will begin early operations across 10 U.S. states in 2026. Participating states include Arizona, Florida, Idaho, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Oklahoma, Oregon, Texas, and Utah. Established by Presidential Executive Order, the eIPP program allows Joby to conduct operations before receiving FAA type certification. The initiative streamlines regulatory coordination by bringing together the FAA, DOT, and local authorities. CEO JoeBen Bevirt stated this is a defining moment for American innovation. Specific state operations include Florida focusing on cargo delivery, passenger transportation, and medical response; New York/New Jersey air taxi operations at the Manhattan heliport; Texas networks connecting Dallas, Austin, San Antonio, and Houston; North Carolina medical operations; and Utah multi-state testing. Flights are expected to commence within 90 days of finalizing Other Transaction Authority contracts. Joby aims to scale production to four aircraft monthly in 2027.
